title: alectryon excelsus subsp. excelsus
intro: Alectryon excelsus subsp. excelsus, also known as the 'Cassowary plum' or 'Barringtonia', is a species of flowering plant in the family Lecythidaceae. The plant is native to the tropical regions of Australia and has been traditionally used in medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Alectryon excelsus subsp. excelsus include reducing fever, treating wounds and skin conditions, and acting as an antiseptic. The plant is typically prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.
